label: Russia invades Ukraine
description: Russian forces enter Ukraine. European gas markets enter sustained crisis; TTF surges from ~80 to over 200 EUR/MWh within weeks. EU begins emergency storage mandate discussions.

label: Nord Stream indefinite shutdown
description: Gazprom halts Nord Stream 1 flows indefinitely, citing turbine maintenance. TTF peaks near 350 EUR/MWh. Germany declares gas emergency. EU begins demand reduction measures.

label: EU storage mandate enters force
description: EU Regulation 2022/1032 requires member states to fill gas storage to 90% by November 1 each year. Structural floor placed under injection-season demand.

label: Ukraine-Russia transit agreement expires
description: Five-year Ukraine-Russia gas transit agreement expires. Remaining Russian pipeline volumes via Ukraine cease. Small residual flows to Slovakia, Austria, Hungary affected.

label: US tariff threats on EU imports
description: US administration signals broad tariffs on European goods. Market concern over retaliatory measures affecting US LNG export agreements and cargo diversion.

label: Norwegian Asgard/Troll maintenance
description: Scheduled maintenance on Norwegian Asgard transport system and Troll field reduces Norwegian pipeline export capacity by approximately 15% for three weeks.

label: Cold spell: Northern Europe
description: Sustained cold weather across Scandinavia and Northern Europe drives heating demand above seasonal norms. Norwegian hydro reservoir drawdown accelerates.

label: US tariff escalation
description: US formalises 25% tariffs on European steel and aluminium. EU considers counter-measures including LNG offtake reviews. TTF reacts to uncertainty over US LNG cargo commitments.

label: Norwegian Troll outage
description: Unplanned outage at Troll field reduces Norwegian gas exports by approximately 50 mcm/day for ten days. One of the largest unplanned reductions since 2022.

label: US-Iran escalation ('Epic Fury')
description: US military operation in the Strait of Hormuz region triggers fears over LNG transit disruption. TTF rises approximately 40% in three days on supply shock concerns. Qatar and Australian LNG cargoes rerouted. Markets reassess Hormuz transit risk premium.

label: Qatar LNG diversion
description: Qatar Petroleum diverts three LNG cargoes from Europe to Asia following JKM-TTF spread widening. European LNG sendout falls 12% week-on-week.

label: EU storage mandate confirmed at 90%
description: EU regulation extended requiring member states to reach 90% storage fill by November 1. The 80% target under EU 2022/1032 was raised to 90% for the 2023-2025+ period. In 2025, EU storage reached 83% by October 1 and met the 90% mandate by November 1.
